automotive-ev-battery.title: DAF begins series production of electric XG models
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Dutch truck manufacturer DAF has commenced series production of its XG Electric and XG+ Electric models at its Eindhoven facility, marking a significant milestone in commercial vehicle electrification. The first unit, an XG+ tractor, will be delivered to German logistics provider Hellmold & Plank.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Global battery market grows by 9.1% in Q1/2026
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The global electric vehicle battery market expanded by 9.1% in the first quarter of 2026, with production dominated by Chinese, South Korean, and Japanese manufacturers. CATL maintains its market leadership position, followed by BYD and LG Energy Solution.

automotive-ev-battery.title: ABB presents charging system for 100+ charge points and 10 MW output
automotive-ev-battery.summary: ABB E-mobility unveiled the OM X-Series charging system at the ACT Expo in Las Vegas, featuring support for over 100 charging points with configurable output ranging from 800 kW to 10 MW. The system targets fleet operators and charging point operators with demanding infrastructure requirements.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Geely reportedly to acquire parts of Ford's Valencia plant
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese automaker Geely has reportedly agreed to purchase the Body 3 assembly hall at Ford's Almussafes plant in Valencia, Spain, with plans to manufacture an electrified vehicle for the European market. The agreement may also include production of a derivative model for Ford.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electric Mercedes C-Class starts at €67,711
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz has opened order books for the new all-electric C-Class sedan approximately two weeks following its official unveiling. In Germany, the C 400 4MATIC variant begins at €67,711 including value-added tax.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Ballard to supply fuel cell modules for new Solaris H2 bus
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Canadian fuel cell supplier Ballard Power Systems has secured a contract to provide fuel cell modules for the next generation of hydrogen buses from Polish bus manufacturer Solaris. The partnership will utilize Ballard's new FCmove-SC fuel cell module in future city bus models.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Rocsys presents charging robot for ports and logistics fleets
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Dutch startup Rocsys unveiled the S2, its second-generation charging robot designed for autonomous vehicle charging in ports and logistics facilities. The new model features a more compact design, enhanced range of motion, and improved durability for harsh outdoor operating conditions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Fraunhofer ISI: Study sees German automotive industry further advanced in EV transition
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A new strategy paper based on automotive executive surveys indicates that Germany's electric mobility transition is more advanced than commonly perceived, according to research from Fraunhofer ISI. The study reveals ongoing industry divisions regarding the pace and direction of the electrification transformation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EU approval for Tesla FSD remains uncertain
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla is seeking European Union-wide approval for its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) driver assistance system following its approval in the Netherlands. However, the approval initiative faces regulatory headwinds, with multiple national authorities reportedly expressing concerns about the technology's implementation across the EU.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Vienna's Hydrogen Bus Failure Is A Warning To Transit Agencies
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Seven of Vienna's ten new hydrogen buses are currently out of service due to CaetanoBus's inability to supply common spare parts such as door compressors and blind-spot monitoring systems. The supply chain failure highlights critical procurement considerations for transit agencies implementing hydrogen bus fleets.

finance-markets.title: US prosecutors charge 30 with insider trading on law firm deal materials
finance-markets.summary: The US Justice Department has charged 30 individuals with insider trading in a scheme involving lawyers from elite Wall Street firms who provided confidential transaction details to facilitate illegal trading. The case represents a significant enforcement action targeting sophisticated financial crime involving material non-public information.

finance-markets.title: US fuel exports hit record in boon for oil companies and threat to Trump
finance-markets.summary: US fuel exports have reached record levels amid a surge in buying from Asia and Europe, particularly following concerns about potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The development benefits US oil companies but may complicate Trump administration energy policy objectives.

finance-markets.title: The leverage layer cake behind HSBC's private credit losses
finance-markets.summary: HSBC incurred a $400 million loss related to the collapsed mortgage provider MFS despite not lending directly to the company, highlighting the complex layered leverage structures in private credit markets. The incident illustrates broader risks in the private credit sector as it has expanded rapidly.

finance-markets.title: Chinese AI start-up DeepSeek nears $45bn valuation
finance-markets.summary: Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek is approaching a $45 billion valuation in ongoing fundraising discussions, with investors including Tencent seeking stakes in the rapidly growing AI laboratory. The valuation surge reflects intense competition and investor appetite in the global AI sector.

finance-markets.title: MetLife's Improved PE Returns Help Profit Beat Expectations
finance-markets.summary: MetLife exceeded Wall Street profit expectations in first-quarter earnings, driven by strong gains from its private equity portfolio as the insurer continues to benefit from robust private equity returns. The result demonstrates the contribution of alternative asset returns to financial services earnings.

finance-markets.title: Gold Holds Hefty Gain as US-Iran Truce Hopes Ease Inflation Fear
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices stabilized following their largest daily gain since late March as optimism over a potential US-Iran deal eased inflation concerns and sent oil prices sharply lower. The commodity's strength reflects market assessment of geopolitical risk reduction and its implications for inflation outlook.

finance-markets.title: Surveillance Firm Hawkeye 360 Said to Raise $416 Million in IPO
finance-markets.summary: Satellite-based signals intelligence provider Hawkeye 360 is raising $416 million in a US initial public offering priced at the top of the marketed range. The IPO reflects growing demand for advanced surveillance and intelligence services among US government agencies.

finance-markets.title: Airbus wins $19bn order in Canada's biggest ever aircraft deal
finance-markets.summary: Airbus has secured a $19 billion order for 150 A220-300 aircraft from AirAsia in Canada's largest-ever aircraft procurement deal, delivering a significant boost to Canadian aerospace manufacturing. The transaction underscores strong demand in the commercial aviation sector and supports North American supply chain activity.

finance-markets.title: RBNZ's Frequent Remit Adjustments Risk Policy Error, OECD Warns
finance-markets.summary: The OECD has cautioned New Zealand that frequent adjustments to the Reserve Bank's remit increase the risk of monetary policy mistakes and inconsistency. The warning highlights concerns about policy predictability and the need for stable central banking frameworks.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX to rent data centre capacity to Anthropic
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX will provide data centre capacity to artificial intelligence start-up Anthropic as the company races to expand computing power to support its rapid growth. The arrangement reflects the acute demand for specialized infrastructure resources across the competitive AI sector.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft's AI data center push is colliding with its clean power goals
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft's aggressive expansion of data centers to support AI infrastructure is creating tension with the company's sustainability commitments and clean energy objectives. The expansion represents a critical conflict between near-term AI capabilities and long-term environmental goals that could reshape corporate climate pledges across the industry.

tech-ai.title: Barry Diller trusts Sam Altman. But 'trust is irrelevant' as AGI nears, he says
tech-ai.summary: IAC chairman Barry Diller expressed confidence in OpenAI CEO Sam Altman while cautioning that trust becomes meaningless as artificial general intelligence approaches. Diller emphasized the need for robust guardrails and governance structures to manage the unpredictable risks posed by AGI development.

tech-ai.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn't trust Sam Altman's words
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI's former CTO Mira Murati testified under oath that CEO Sam Altman misrepresented safety protocols for a new AI model during the ongoing Musk v. Altman trial. Murati stated that Altman falsely claimed the company's legal department had determined the model did not require safety board review.

tech-ai.title: Is xAI a neocloud now?
tech-ai.summary: Analysis suggests that Elon Musk's xAI may be pivoting toward data center infrastructure services rather than focusing primarily on AI model development. The shift indicates xAI's real competitive advantage may lie in building and operating advanced computational facilities for AI training.

tech-ai.title: AI evaluation startup Braintrust confirms breach, tells every customer to rotate sensitive keys
tech-ai.summary: Braintrust, an AI software development platform, disclosed a security breach affecting its Amazon cloud environment and instructed all customers to rotate their API keys immediately. The incident highlights ongoing security vulnerabilities in the AI infrastructure supply chain.

tech-ai.title: Snap says its $400M deal with Perplexity 'amicably ended'
tech-ai.summary: Snap announced the termination of its previously announced $400 million partnership with AI search engine Perplexity that would have integrated the service into Snapchat. The deal's dissolution marks a significant shift in Snap's AI strategy following the November 2025 announcement.

tech-ai.title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
tech-ai.summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president, provided rare public insight into the contentious negotiations between Elon Musk and OpenAI's founders that led to Musk's departure from the company. The testimony reveals the intense business dynamics at a world-changing AI organization during its critical early stages.

tech-ai.title: Insurance startup Corgi hits $1.3B valuation 4 months after its Series A
tech-ai.summary: Insurance technology startup Corgi announced a $160 million Series B funding round led by TCV, valuing the company at $1.3 billion just four months after completing its Series A. The rapid valuation increase demonstrates strong investor confidence in AI-driven insurance solutions.

tech-ai.title: Robinhood's venture fund IPO attracted 150,000+ retail investors, CEO says
tech-ai.summary: Robinhood reported that its new venture fund attracted over 150,000 retail investors, providing individual access to stakes in private tech companies including OpenAI, Stripe, Databricks, and Oura. The fund democratizes venture capital exposure to high-value pre-IPO technology companies.

tech-ai.title: Google shuts down Project Mariner
tech-ai.summary: Google discontinued Project Mariner, an experimental web automation tool that could perform up to ten tasks across the internet for users, transferring its technology to other Google products. The shutdown ends the public testing phase of the autonomous browsing agent initiative.

science-space.title: NASA's Roman Poised to Transform Hunt for Elusive Neutron Stars
science-space.summary: A new study published in Astronomy and Astrophysics indicates that NASA's upcoming Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will be capable of detecting neutron stars throughout the Milky Way galaxy, despite their previously invisible nature. Using detailed simulations, researchers demonstrate that the Roman telescope could revolutionize the search for these remnants of massive stellar explosions.

science-space.title: Unlocking the Mystery of X-ray Dots
science-space.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has discovered a new class of mysterious X-ray objects that could explain the nature of hundreds or potentially thousands of similar objects identified since the James Webb Space Telescope began operations. This breakthrough may help astronomers understand a previously unexplained astronomical phenomenon.

science-space.title: 3 puzzles of our universe could be solved with this new dark matter theory
science-space.summary: A novel dark matter theory featuring self-interacting particles presents a unified solution to three separate cosmic mysteries affecting our understanding of the universe's structure and evolution. This theoretical framework could significantly advance cosmological research and address longstanding questions in astrophysics.

science-space.title: Scientists created one of the largest simulations of our universe ever — about the size of 500,000 HD movies
science-space.summary: The FLAMINGO project has produced one of the most comprehensive simulations of the universe to date, enabling scientists to explore the evolution of galaxies, dark matter, and cosmic structures over billions of years. This computational achievement provides new tools for understanding large-scale structure formation in the cosmos.

science-space.title: How an exoplanet odd couple survived by traveling in from the cold together
science-space.summary: Using the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers analyzed the atmospheric composition of a mini-Neptune exoplanet and determined it formed much farther from its star than its current location, providing insights into the formation and migration patterns of mini-Neptune systems. This discovery may explain the origins of numerous similar exoplanets discovered throughout the galaxy.

science-space.title: New NASA Technology Mimics Extreme Cold of the Lunar Night
science-space.summary: NASA researchers have developed technology capable of reproducing the extreme temperatures encountered on the Moon and Mars to test materials for future space missions. Understanding material behavior in frigid environments is critical for developing reliable equipment for lunar and planetary exploration.

science-space.title: NASA Sets Coverage for SpaceX 34th Station Resupply Launch, Arrival
science-space.summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting May 12 for the launch of the 34th commercial resupply mission to the International Space Station, carrying approximately 6,500 pounds of cargo aboard a Dragon spacecraft. The mission represents the ongoing effort to sustain critical research and operations aboard the orbital outpost.

science-space.title: Europe's 1st reusable spacecraft 'Space Rider' clears key hurdles on the road to launch
science-space.summary: The European Space Agency's Space Rider, a novel reusable spacecraft concept, has successfully passed critical testing and qualification milestones necessary for achieving flight readiness. This development represents progress toward Europe's goal of establishing independent reusable spaceflight capabilities.

science-space.title: 'Whatever Russia is testing, it's sophisticated': 2 Russian satellites get within 10 feet of each other in orbit
science-space.summary: Two Russian satellites recently achieved a close approach of approximately 10 feet in orbit, demonstrating advanced orbital maneuvering and rendezvous capabilities. The demonstration suggests Russia continues to develop sophisticated space technology with potential military or technical applications.

science-space.title: NASA's Dryden Aeronautical Test Range Supports Flight, Space Missions
science-space.summary: The Dryden Aeronautical Test Range at NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, provides critical communications, tracking, and data services essential for experimental aircraft research and flight missions. This infrastructure is fundamental to NASA's aeronautics and space technology advancement efforts.

world-news.title: Iran considering US proposal to end war, official says
world-news.summary: The White House is reportedly advancing negotiations with Iran toward a 14-point memorandum of understanding that could end the ongoing conflict. This development represents a significant shift in U.S.-Iran diplomatic engagement with potential global ramifications.

world-news.title: Russia tells diplomats to leave Kyiv in case Moscow launches mass strikes
world-news.summary: Russia has issued a warning to diplomats to evacuate Kyiv amid threats of retaliatory strikes if Ukraine attacks Russian territory during Victory Day commemorations. The escalation signals heightened military tensions and potential for expanded conflict in Eastern Europe.

world-news.title: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since Hezbollah ceasefire
world-news.summary: Israel conducted its first military strike on Beirut since the mid-April Hezbollah ceasefire, targeting a senior Hezbollah figure in the Lebanese capital. The strike marks a potential escalation and breach of the fragile ceasefire agreement.

world-news.title: North Korea says it is not bound by any treaty on nuclear non-proliferation
world-news.summary: Pyongyang declared that its status as a nuclear-armed state will not be altered by external pressure or diplomatic claims, explicitly rejecting constraints from international nuclear non-proliferation agreements. The statement underscores North Korea's defiant stance on nuclear weapons development.

world-news.title: In rare push, US lawmakers demand transparency on Israel nuclear capability
world-news.summary: U.S. lawmakers are calling for greater transparency regarding Israel's nuclear weapons capabilities, arguing that the current policy of deliberate ambiguity increases risks during the ongoing regional conflict. The rare congressional push reflects growing concern about nuclear proliferation in the Middle East.

world-news.title: What are China's surprise gains in the war on Iran?
world-news.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi visited Beijing seeking Chinese support for a potential nuclear deal with the United States, highlighting China's emerging strategic role in Middle Eastern diplomacy. The engagement underscores shifting geopolitical alignments and Beijing's influence in regional negotiations.

world-news.title: Oil prices drop and stock markets rise after reports of deal to end Iran war
world-news.summary: Global financial markets responded positively to reports of potential U.S.-Iran negotiations, with oil prices declining and equities rising on optimism over conflict resolution. The market reaction reflects investor confidence in reduced geopolitical risk.

world-news.title: Rising Fuel costs overshadowing agenda for ASEAN summit in the Philippines
world-news.summary: ASEAN leaders convened in the Philippines as rising fuel costs emerged as the dominant concern for residents, threatening to overshadow the formal diplomatic agenda. The economic pressures highlight regional vulnerability to global energy market fluctuations.

world-news.title: To stay or risk the 'Road of Death' - Ukrainian civilians trapped in frontline city
world-news.summary: Civilians in the Ukrainian city of Oleshky report being cut off from essential supplies of food and medicine for months due to their location on the frontline. The humanitarian crisis illustrates the severe civilian impact of the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict.

world-news.title: Argentina investigates link to deadly hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
world-news.summary: Argentina is investigating a deadly hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship off the Cape Verde coast that has resulted in three deaths. The incident raises international public health concerns regarding disease transmission on commercial vessels operating across maritime boundaries.

health-biotech.title: Sanofi asks to pull diabetes drug out of FDA voucher program after political appointee interfered with review
health-biotech.summary: Sanofi has requested the FDA withdraw its type 1 diabetes drug from FDA Commissioner Marty Makary's expedited review program, citing concerns over political interference in the drug review process. The incident raises significant questions about the integrity of FDA regulatory decisions and the appropriate role of political appointees in scientific review procedures.

health-biotech.title: Administration report on most favored nation drug pricing raises new details — and questions
health-biotech.summary: A new administration report on most-favored nation drug pricing policy provides additional details on implementation while simultaneously raising questions about the program's practical effectiveness and impact on pharmaceutical innovation. The report represents a significant shift in federal drug pricing policy with wide-ranging implications for the industry.

health-biotech.title: How a global effort to explore the 'dark proteome' is upending our understanding of human disease
health-biotech.summary: An international research team has discovered thousands of previously uncharacterized mini-proteins, including those involved in cell division and DNA repair, with several unique variants found in cancer cells. This breakthrough in proteomics research could fundamentally reshape understanding of disease mechanisms and open new therapeutic targets.

health-biotech.title: Amgen, AbbVie say IRA negotiations impacted Q1 sales
health-biotech.summary: Major pharmaceutical companies Amgen and AbbVie reported that the Inflation Reduction Act's first round of drug price negotiations, which took effect in January, negatively impacted their first-quarter sales. The reports mark the initial financial consequences of federal drug pricing legislation on large pharmaceutical manufacturers.

health-biotech.title: Bayer to buy Perfuse Therapeutics for $300M upfront
health-biotech.summary: Bayer is acquiring ophthalmology biotech Perfuse Therapeutics for $300 million upfront to obtain its mid-stage program targeting major causes of blindness. The deal marks Bayer's first pharmaceutical M&A transaction and reflects strategic interest in expanding its ophthalmology portfolio.

health-biotech.title: Lilly plans new $4.5B manufacturing investment in Indiana
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly announced a $4.5 billion manufacturing investment across Indiana facilities, strengthening its commitment to domestic pharmaceutical production. The investment reflects broader industry trends toward increased U.S.-based manufacturing capacity among major pharmaceutical companies.

health-biotech.title: Madrigal's Rezdiffra beats estimates; Gilead cuts jobs; Banyan debuts
health-biotech.summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals reported first-quarter Rezdiffra sales of $311 million, exceeding analyst expectations by approximately $10 million for the metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis drug. The strong performance demonstrates continued commercial traction for the recently approved therapy.

health-biotech.title: CellCentric raises $220M to get multiple myeloma pill to market
health-biotech.summary: CellCentric secured a $220 million Series D funding round to advance its multiple myeloma oral medication toward commercialization for patients who have exhausted other treatment options. The financing demonstrates investor confidence in the transatlantic biotech's pipeline development.

health-biotech.title: Color Health moving deeper into cancer services, complete with virtual 'tumor boards'
health-biotech.summary: Color Health is expanding beyond cancer screening to provide comprehensive cancer care coordination services, including AI-driven virtual tumor boards. The move addresses a market gap by integrating screening, diagnosis, and treatment coordination into a unified cancer care platform.

health-biotech.title: Avalo scores Phase 2 success in comeback bid, raises $375M
health-biotech.summary: Avalo Therapeutics achieved positive Phase 2 trial results for an experimental inflammatory skin disorder treatment while securing $375 million in funding to support its turnaround strategy. The clinical success and financing validate the immunology biotech's renewed development approach.

cybersecurity.title: Palo Alto PAN-OS Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables Remote Code Execution
cybersecurity.summary: Palo Alto Networks has disclosed a critical buffer overflow vulnerability in PAN-OS software (CVE-2026-0300) with a CVSS score of 9.3 that enables unauthenticated remote code execution and is currently being exploited in the wild. Organizations with internet-exposed User-ID Authentication Portals face immediate risk from this actively weaponized vulnerability.

cybersecurity.title: 'CanisterWorm' Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
cybersecurity.summary: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group has deployed a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and targets systems configured with Iran's time zone or Farsi language settings to wipe data. The attack represents an attempt by cybercriminals to exploit geopolitical tensions for malicious purposes.

cybersecurity.title: MuddyWater Uses Microsoft Teams to Steal Credentials in False Flag Ransomware Attack
cybersecurity.summary: The Iranian state-sponsored hacking group MuddyWater has conducted a false flag ransomware attack leveraging social engineering techniques via Microsoft Teams to initiate infections and steal credentials. The operation demonstrates the group's continued use of legitimate communication platforms for initial compromise.

cybersecurity.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed an intrusion campaign using the CloudZ remote access tool and a previously undocumented Pheno plugin to steal credentials and one-time passwords via the Windows Phone Link feature. The attack exploits the PC-to-smartphone bridge functionality to bypass multi-factor authentication controls.

cybersecurity.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
cybersecurity.summary: A Brazilian anti-DDoS security firm has been implicated in enabling a botnet responsible for extensive DDoS attacks against competing network operators in the country. The company's CEO attributed the malicious activity to a security breach perpetrated by competitors seeking to damage the firm's reputation.

cybersecurity.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
cybersecurity.summary: According to Gartner's inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, enterprise adoption of artificial intelligence agents is accelerating faster than organizations can implement governance and policy controls. Security teams face the challenge of managing autonomous AI tools that have already been deployed within corporate networks with limited oversight.

cybersecurity.title: Mirai-Based xlabs_v1 Botnet Exploits ADB to Hijack IoT Devices for DDoS Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have identified a new Mirai-derived botnet called xlabs_v1 that exploits internet-exposed Android Debug Bridge services to compromise IoT devices for distributed denial-of-service attacks. The discovery was made after researchers identified exposed directories on a Netherlands-hosted server containing botnet infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released security patches addressing at least 77 vulnerabilities across Windows operating systems and other software products in its monthly Patch Tuesday update. Unlike February which contained five zero-day vulnerabilities, this month's release contains no zero-day flaws but still includes several patches warranting prompt attention from enterprise administrators.

cybersecurity.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
cybersecurity.summary: Autonomous AI-based assistants with access to user computers, files, and online services are gaining popularity among developers and IT workers while simultaneously creating novel security challenges for organizations. These powerful tools are blurring traditional security boundaries between data and code, trusted employees and potential insider threats.

cybersecurity.title: Trellix Source Code Breach Highlights Growing Supply Chain Threats
cybersecurity.summary: A breach exposing Trellix source code underscores the escalating risk of supply chain attacks against security vendors. Such breaches can reveal critical implementation details including the location of security controls and detection mechanisms, providing attackers with significant tactical advantages.

startups-vc.title: DeepSeek could hit $45B valuation from its first investment round
startups-vc.summary: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek has gained significant prominence in early 2025 after launching a large language model that achieves comparable results to OpenAI and Anthropic models while using a fraction of the compute power and cost. The startup could reach a $45 billion valuation in its first investment round, signaling a major shift in AI development economics.

startups-vc.title: Insurance startup Corgi hits $1.3B valuation 4 months after its Series A
startups-vc.summary: Insurance technology startup Corgi announced a $160 million Series B funding round led by TCV, bringing its valuation to $1.3 billion just four months after completing its Series A. The rapid valuation increase reflects strong market demand and investor confidence in the insurtech sector.

startups-vc.title: Khosla-backed robotics startup Genesis AI has gone full stack, demo shows
startups-vc.summary: Genesis AI, a Khosla Ventures-backed startup that raised a $105 million seed round to develop foundational AI for robotics, unveiled its first model GENE-26.5 alongside demonstrations of robotic hands performing complex tasks. The company has expanded beyond foundational models to deliver an integrated robotics solution.

startups-vc.title: Frontier Labs And Robotics Companies Again Top List Of New Unicorns In April
startups-vc.summary: Twenty-eight companies joined the Crunchbase Unicorn Board in April 2026, with robotics startups and frontier labs leading the unicorn creation trends for the second consecutive month. This marks a significant concentration of innovation in these two technology categories.

startups-vc.title: A 20-minute pitch wins Indian startup Pronto backing from Lachy Groom
startups-vc.summary: Indian logistics startup Pronto secured investment from prominent venture capitalist Lachy Groom following a brief 20-minute pitch, as the company scales to 26,000 daily bookings. The market for logistics services in India is projected to reach $18 billion, positioning Pronto within a rapidly growing sector.

startups-vc.title: AI Is Rewriting What Investors Should Look For In Early Startup Teams
startups-vc.summary: As artificial intelligence tools democratize product development capabilities, technical expertise alone no longer serves as a competitive differentiator for early-stage startups, forcing investors to reassess evaluation criteria. The shift raises critical questions about what constitutes sustainable competitive advantage when product development barriers are lowered.

startups-vc.title: Palantir Q1 2026 Has Broken the Enterprise Software Mold. Again. Revenue Growth Accelerates to 85% at $6.5B+ ARR
startups-vc.summary: Palantir reported exceptional Q1 2026 results with 85% year-over-year revenue growth reaching $1.633 billion, continuing to redefine enterprise software performance standards. The company's accelerating growth trajectory at scale challenges conventional wisdom about mature software company growth limitations.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Supabase Execs Were So Impressed With Dreambase, They Became Investors In Its $3.7M Round
startups-vc.summary: Dreambase, an AI-powered analytics platform designed to enable data-driven decision-making without dedicated data teams, raised $3.7 million in funding with Supabase executives participating as investors. The backing from established startup leaders signals strong validation for AI-powered analytics solutions.

startups-vc.title: The No. 1 Reason M&A Deals Fail Before They Even Start
startups-vc.summary: Misaligned founder expectations represent the primary barrier to successful M&A transactions, with common issues preventing deal closure before negotiations advance. Understanding the structural realities of acquisition processes is critical for founders evaluating exit opportunities.

startups-vc.title: Marc Lore says that AI will soon enable anyone to open a restaurant
startups-vc.summary: Wonder, led by entrepreneur Marc Lore, is developing robotic kitchen technology positioned as AI-powered "restaurant factories" that would allow anyone to launch virtual food brands through simple prompts. The vision represents a significant democratization of restaurant operations and brand creation.

crypto-web3.title: AI agents becoming more relevant than humans by 2035 has Big Tech 'terrified', says Hoskinson
crypto-web3.summary: The founder and CEO of Cardano’s Input Output said Google, Amazon and Facebook are terrified because AI agents will not behave like humans when confronted with ads.

crypto-web3.title: Grant Cardone says bitcoin-real estate strategy could outperform REITs, adds more BTC to treasury
crypto-web3.summary: The real-estate mogul said the hybrid model brings new users into crypto and challenges traditional real estate structures.

crypto-web3.title: Nasdaq's president says the SEC’s new crypto stance is letting markets 'build' again
crypto-web3.summary: Nasdaq’s Tal Cohen said a friendlier SEC is giving crypto firms and exchanges room to experiment with tokenization and digital market infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Wall Street's clearinghouse seeks 'high-performance' blockchains to tokenize corporate actions
crypto-web3.summary: DTCC CEO Frank La Salla said the clearing giant is working with layer-1 blockchains to bring millions in corporate actions like dividend payments onchain, but challenges remain.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto’s mainstream moment has arrived, say industry leaders speaking at Consensus Miami
crypto-web3.summary: Executives from Binance, Revolut and Circle say crypto is evolving beyond speculation into the financial infrastructure powering payments, remittances and broader global access.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin’s post-quantum migration will be harder than Taproot and needs to start now, Project Eleven CEO says
crypto-web3.summary: Alex Pruden said the asymmetry between acting on a post-quantum signature scheme today and waiting for certainty about quantum-computing hardware timelines means Bitcoin developers should move from research into production.

crypto-web3.title: Reid Hoffman says NFTs may make a comeback as AI agents strain online identity
crypto-web3.summary: The Greylock partner and LinkedIn co-founder said autonomous agents will need crypto-based trust systems to transact across the open internet.

crypto-web3.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs solved access, but custody, advisors and plumbing still lag, panelists say
crypto-web3.summary: Senior figures from CoinShares, Calamos, ProShares and Flow Traders flagged Coinbase-heavy custody concentration, modest advisor uptake and creation-flow inefficiency as being among the next set of problems to solve.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. Bitcoin Reserve update coming in 'next few weeks," White House adviser says
crypto-web3.summary: White House digital-assets adviser Patrick Witt cited a recent exploit involving assets held by the U.S. Marshals as proof federal crypto holdings need safeguarding.

crypto-web3.title: White House targets July 4 for Clarity Act passage, says crypto adviser Patrick Witt
crypto-web3.summary: The executive director of the President's Council of Advisors for Digital Assets said the Senate Banking Committee hearing will happen this month on market structure. bill.

energy-climate.title: Firm solar and storage costs fall to $54/MWh, says IRENA
energy-climate.summary: The International Renewable Energy Agency reports that solar and wind paired with battery storage now deliver power at lower cost than new fossil fuel generation in high-quality resource regions. This milestone represents a significant economic shift favoring renewable energy deployment globally.

energy-climate.title: There are now more than 2 million UK solar installations
energy-climate.summary: The United Kingdom reached a milestone of over 2 million solar installations in March 2026, driven by increased rooftop and large-scale deployment with volumes returning to levels unseen since 2012. Further government support is expected to drive even higher monthly installation rates going forward.

energy-climate.title: Angola switches on Africa's largest off-grid solar-plus-storage park
energy-climate.summary: Angola has launched Africa's largest off-grid solar-plus-storage facility, consisting of 31.85 MW of solar capacity paired with 75.26 MWh of battery storage, bringing electricity access to over 90,000 people in eastern Angola. This project demonstrates renewable energy's potential for rural electrification in Africa.

energy-climate.title: Cropped 6 May 2026: Forest loss falls | Deforestation regulations | Saving 'India's Galapagos'
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's curated digest highlights declining forest loss trends alongside emerging deforestation regulations and conservation efforts in biodiversity hotspots. The coverage addresses critical climate and land-use policy developments affecting global carbon cycles.

energy-climate.title: Fraunhofer ISE opens perovskite-silicon tandem scale-up lab
energy-climate.summary: Germany's Fraunhofer Institute for Solar Energy Systems has opened a dedicated laboratory for scaling perovskite-silicon tandem solar cell designs using industry-standard manufacturing processes. The facility represents a significant step toward commercializing next-generation photovoltaic technology with higher efficiency potential.

energy-climate.title: ENGIE achieves key milestones in French offshore wind projects
energy-climate.summary: ENGIE, through its Ocean Winds subsidiary, has brought the Yeu and Noirmoutier Offshore Wind Farm into full commercial operation in France. The project represents progress in Europe's offshore wind expansion and energy transition objectives.

energy-climate.title: Egypt's National Bank takes 20% stake in Scatec's Obelisk project
energy-climate.summary: Egypt's National Bank acquired a 20% equity stake in Scatec ASA's 1.1 GW Obelisk solar and battery storage project, reducing the Norwegian developer's economic interest while maintaining operational control. The investment signals growing Middle Eastern financial participation in large-scale renewable energy infrastructure.

energy-climate.title: PV module recycling technologies 'progressing', says IEA-PVPS
energy-climate.summary: The International Energy Agency's Photovoltaic Power Systems Programme reports measurable advancements in solar module recycling, including higher material recovery rates, improved process yields, and enhanced output purity. This progress addresses the circular economy challenge posed by aging solar installations worldwide.

energy-climate.title: Germany's renewable sector: Reform, uncertainty and the race against the clock
energy-climate.summary: Germany's energy transition focus has shifted from expansion toward integration as Berlin implements reforms to manage increasing renewable capacity on the grid. The article examines policy challenges and infrastructure requirements for accommodating higher renewable penetration.

energy-climate.title: Norwegian startup testing hybrid solar, wave, wind system
energy-climate.summary: Norway-based Flex2Future has begun testing a scaled prototype of its integrated offshore energy system combining solar, wave, and wind generation in collaboration with research firm SINTEF. The startup claims the hybrid system can deliver power at competitive per-kilowatt-hour costs.

asia-pacific.title: North Korea says nuclear status ‘will not change’ despite external pressure
asia-pacific.summary:
North Korea’s UN envoy said his country was not bound by the non-proliferation treaty (NPT) on nuclear weapons and external pressure would not change its status as a nuclear-armed state, official media reported on Thursday.
Pyongyang threatened to withdraw from the treaty in 1993 and formally did so in 2003. It has since conducted six nuclear tests – prompting a raft of international sanctions – and is believed to possess dozens of nuclear warheads.
Signatories of the NPT have been holding a...

asia-pacific.title: Do artificial sweeteners cause inheritable biological changes?
asia-pacific.summary:
A new study from the University of Chile suggests that common artificial sweeteners may trigger biological changes that are passed down to future generations.
The research focused on sucralose and stevia, two of the world’s most widely used non-nutritive sweeteners (NNS).
The additives could alter gut microbiota, gene expression and metabolism in mice, with some effects persisting in their first- and second-generation offspring, even in offspring who were never directly exposed to the...

asia-pacific.title: Falling sales to widen profit gap between China’s EV makers and battery suppliers
asia-pacific.summary:
Profit trajectories between automotive assemblers and electric vehicle (EV) battery producers in China are set to diverge further due to lacklustre car sales and buoyant demand for energy-storage systems.
Leading players, including China’s EV battery king Contemporary Amperex Technology Ltd (CATL), would continue to attract buying interest in their shares, spurred by improved profitability, according to analysts.
They added that flat vehicle prices, coupled with a dip in new car sales, had...

asia-pacific.title: In China, number of clothing buttons holds meaning; specific counts assigned for the dead
asia-pacific.summary:
The revival of Chinese hanfu, or traditional dress of the Han people, in recent years has drawn attention to the complicated etiquette surrounding buttons.
As the Xinzhongshi, or neo-Chinese style clothing, took over the market in recent years, some people complained online that they had bought clothes that would bring bad luck.
The telltale sign was the number of buttons on a garment.

In some Chinese regions, a folk belief existed which held that if the number of buttons is four or six, this..

asia-pacific.title: Traffic accidents involving animals in Hong Kong surge 11-fold in 4 years
asia-pacific.summary:
Traffic accidents involving animals in Hong Kong have skyrocketed more than 11-fold over the past four years, with advocacy groups attributing it to a wave of pet abandonments and the blurring of urban-rural boundaries caused by large-scale development.
The latest data submitted to the Legislative Council on Wednesday showed reports of animal-related traffic accidents received by police surged from 36 cases in 2021 to 416 in 2025.
However, authorities do not maintain a district-level breakdown..

asia-pacific.title: Is North Korea’s constitutional shift a move towards ‘peaceful coexistence’ with South?
asia-pacific.summary:
North Korea has removed references to “national reunification” from its constitution and newly defined its territory as land bordering South Korea, a move analysts say may signal Pyongyang’s intent to avoid direct conflict with Seoul.
The constitutional overhaul is widely seen as aligning with the North’s evolving stance towards Seoul – shifting away from reunification and towards a more formalised state-to-state relationship.
Pyongyang took more than two years to revise the constitution after..

asia-pacific.title: Note linked to Epstein’s first suspected suicide bid released by US judge
asia-pacific.summary:
A note Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate claimed he found after the financier’s first suspected jail suicide attempt was made public on Wednesday after it had been sealed and locked in a courthouse vault for nearly five years as part of an unrelated legal dispute.
US District Judge Kenneth Karas in White Plains, New York, ordered the note’s release after The New York Times petitioned last week to unseal it and other documents in a case involving the cellmate, Nicholas Tartaglione.
Few people had

asia-pacific.title: Trump seeks to halt payment to E. Jean Carroll in hope of eventual Supreme Court win
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump’s lawyer, hoping for an eventual Supreme Court victory, has asked a federal appeal court in New York to temporarily block a long-time columnist from collecting a US$83 million defamation award.
The lawyer, Justin D. Smith, told the 2nd US Circuit Court of Appeals in a filing on Tuesday to stay its decision supporting the award so that Trump will not have to pay writer E. Jean Carroll while he appeals to the high court.
A Manhattan jury awarded Carroll the payout in...

asia-pacific.title: US trial opens over alleged Chinese ‘secret police station’ in New York
asia-pacific.summary:
A trial of a US citizen of Chinese descent accused of creating a covert police station in Manhattan opened on Wednesday, considering evidence of what the US government says is part of Beijing’s bid to expand its influence well beyond Chinese shores.
Lu Jianwang, 64, faces three charges in the US Eastern District Court of New York: acting as an unauthorised agent of China; conspiracy to act as a foreign agent; and obstruction of justice. He is accused of using the fourth floor of a building in...

asia-pacific.title: Will warming Arctic trigger further chill in US-China relations or bring them closer?
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump’s landmark visit to China comes as the Iran war disrupts global energy supplies, fuels economic uncertainty and adds fresh strain to Washington-Beijing ties. In the first part of a series examining how rivalry, interdependence and geopolitical crises are reshaping the relationship between the two powers, we look at the Arctic as an arena for competition.
The Arctic is heating up, both physically and in terms of geopolitics.
As Arctic warming accelerates and sea ice...
